question_text: Which change would have the greatest effect on increasing the strength of an electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Increasing the number of coils in the wire

options.label: B
options.text: Making the iron core slightly longer

options.label: C
options.text: Using thicker copper wire for the coil

options.label: D
options.text: Placing the electromagnet closer to a magnet

correct_answer: Increasing the number of coils in the wire
explanation: The strength of an electromagnet depends on the number of coils, the current flowing through it, and the iron core. Increasing the number of coils has the greatest effect because each additional loop adds to the magnetic field. More coils mean more turns of wire carrying current, which creates a significantly stronger magnetic field compared to small changes in core length or wire thickness.
